_note: Converts a real photograph into a recognizable video-game character using Flux2 Klein dual-reference style transfer. Reference 1 is the real person (identity anchor). Reference 2 is a game-character image (style target). The prompt controls transformation direction. Use the style presets and intensity profiles below to control the output without rewiring the graph.
base_prompt: Transform the real subject into a high-quality video-game character. Preserve recognizable facial identity, face structure, hairstyle, skin tone, body proportions, pose, and core outfit silhouette. Use Reference 2 only for gaming style, costume materials, lighting language, and visual genre. Do not copy the face or body identity from Reference 2. Render the subject with readable game-character materials, cinematic lighting, clean silhouette separation, detailed clothing surfaces, and professional promotional character presentation.

AAA.label: AAA Semi-Realistic
AAA.suffix: AAA semi-realistic game rendering, physically-based materials, subsurface scattering on skin, cinematic depth of field, hero key lighting, clean character silhouette separation, Unreal Engine 5 quality.
AAA.reference_2_guidance: Use a screenshot or promotional render from a AAA game (Horizon, God of War, Final Fantasy XVI).

HERO.label: Stylized Hero Character
HERO.suffix: Stylized hero-shooter character rendering, slightly exaggerated proportions, bold material reads, strong rim lighting, saturated color palette, clean character design language, Overwatch-quality presentation.
HERO.reference_2_guidance: Use a hero-shooter character render (Overwatch, Valorant, Apex Legends).

FANTASY.label: Fantasy RPG
FANTASY.suffix: High-fantasy RPG character rendering, ornate armor details, enchanted material effects, warm dramatic lighting, rich jewel-tone palette, painterly texture quality, Baldur's Gate 3 character quality.
FANTASY.reference_2_guidance: Use a fantasy RPG character render or concept art (Baldur's Gate, Elder Scrolls, Dragon Age).

SCIFI.label: Sci-Fi Operative
SCIFI.suffix: Sci-fi operative character rendering, hard-surface armor panels, holographic interface elements, cool blue-teal lighting, matte-and-gloss material contrast, cybernetic detail accents, Mass Effect character quality.
SCIFI.reference_2_guidance: Use a sci-fi character render (Mass Effect, Halo, Cyberpunk 2077, Destiny).

CEL_SHADED.label: Cel-Shaded Game Character
CEL_SHADED.suffix: Cel-shaded game character rendering, clean ink outlines, flat color zones with sharp shadow boundaries, limited color palette, cartoon material reads, bright saturated lighting, Genshin Impact character quality.
CEL_SHADED.reference_2_guidance: Use a cel-shaded game character (Genshin Impact, Breath of the Wild, Borderlands, Jet Set Radio).

GRITTY.label: Dark Action-Game Character
GRITTY.suffix: Dark action-game character rendering, worn leather and weathered metal textures, desaturated earth tones, harsh directional lighting, gritty surface detail, visible damage and environmental wear, The Last of Us character quality.
GRITTY.reference_2_guidance: Use a dark action game character (The Last of Us, Dark Souls, Resident Evil, Sekiro).

SPLASH_ART.label: Promotional Splash Art
SPLASH_ART.suffix: Promotional splash-art character rendering, dramatic action pose preservation, painterly background, volumetric lighting, heroic composition, saturated key colors, dynamic energy, League of Legends splash art quality.
SPLASH_ART.reference_2_guidance: Use a game splash art or promotional character illustration (League of Legends, Dota 2, fighting game key art).

IDENTITY_LOCK.label: Identity Lock — Maximum Likeness
IDENTITY_LOCK.description: Maximum identity preservation with subtle game conversion. Conservative costume changes. The person is immediately recognizable.
IDENTITY_LOCK.prompt_prefix: Maximally preserve the exact facial identity, precise face proportions, exact hairstyle, and skin tone of the real subject. Apply only subtle game-rendering treatment. Keep the original outfit mostly intact.

BALANCED_GAMING.label: Balanced Gaming — Strong Identity + Obvious Game Style
BALANCED_GAMING.description: Strong identity retention with obvious game-character conversion. Moderate costume and material redesign. The person remains clearly recognizable while looking like a game character.

FULL_CHARACTER_REDESIGN.label: Full Character Redesign — Playable Character
FULL_CHARACTER_REDESIGN.description: Identity remains recognizable but with stronger costume transformation and stylization. More dramatic game-world presentation. The subject looks like a redesigned playable character.
FULL_CHARACTER_REDESIGN.prompt_prefix: Transform the subject into a fully redesigned playable game character. Preserve core facial identity but allow stronger costume redesign, bolder material choices, and more dramatic game-world presentation.

build_prompt: Concatenate: intensity.prompt_prefix + base_prompt + ' ' + style.suffix + ' High detail, clean edges, no extra limbs, no warped hands, no text, no watermark.'

rubric.identity: 1-5 — is the person immediately recognizable from the original photo?
rubric.game_style: 1-5 — does the output clearly look like a video-game character, not a photograph?
rubric.costume_coherence: 1-5 — are materials, armor, and clothing details clean and readable?
rubric.anatomy: 1-5 — correct hands, fingers, limbs, face proportions?
rubric.composition: 1-5 — clean silhouette, good lighting, professional presentation?

failure_guide.face_changed_too_much: Increase ref1_megapixels, decrease ref2_megapixels, lower guidance, strengthen identity language in prompt.
failure_guide.still_looks_like_photo: Increase guidance slightly, use a clearer game-character Reference 2, add stronger material and rendering language to prompt.
failure_guide.chaotic_costume: Reduce ref2_megapixels, use a cleaner reference, simplify prompt to one material system and one genre.
failure_guide.anatomy_broken: Reduce guidance, return to SAFE steps/dimensions, avoid extreme pose references, do not depend on upscale to fix anatomy.
failure_guide.vram_oom: Unload LM Studio models, keep batch size 1, keep SeedVR2 bypassed, keep upscale bypassed, return to safe workflow defaults.
